heroHeadline: PLA De-Escalates to 2 Sorties on Apr 12 (KMT Visit Ends) — China Announces 10-Measure Cross-Strait Package as Cheng Li-wun Departs Beijing; DPP Rejects; Trump-Xi Summit May 14-15 (32 Days)
heroSubtitle: PLA air activity dropped sharply to 2 sorties on April 12 as KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun concluded her six-day China visit and departed Beijing for Taiwan; China's Taiwan Affairs Office simultaneously announced a 10-measure cross-strait package — including resumed individual travel, normalized direct flights, agricultural trade, and a CPC-KMT communication mechanism — all conditioned on the 1992 Consensus; DPP government rejected the measures; KMT welcomed them. PLA visit pattern: 1 (Apr 7) → 17 (Apr 11 post-summit surge) → 2 (Apr 12 visit ends), confirming pressure-and-dialogue dual-track strategy. 32 days to Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14-15); Taiwan's NT$1.25T defense budget still stalled in KMT-controlled legislature.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
